task_completion.description: Open Panoply and load the SST dataset
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open, and 'sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc' is visible in the Datasets or Sources panel.

task_completion.description: Create a geographic map plot for the sea surface temperature (sst) variable
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window is open displaying a map of the 'sst' (Sea Surface Temperature) variable.

task_completion.description: Set the plot time dimension to August
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Panoply plot window's Array(s) tab, the Time dimension is set to index 7 or explicitly shows 'August'.

task_completion.description: Adjust the plot scale to highlight the 28.2°C bleaching threshold
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Scale tab in the Panoply plot window is open with the Min/Max or custom scale adjusted to highlight temperatures around or above 28.2°C, or the color bar on the map clearly reflects this threshold.

task_completion.description: Save the global and hotspot plot images to the ReefStress directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows 'reef_stress_global_aug.png' and 'reef_stress_hotspot.png' successfully saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/ReefStress' directory.

task_completion.description: Create the thermal stress report text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the contents of 'thermal_stress_report.txt' with analysis of the stressed regions, or the file is visible in the '/home/ga/Documents/ReefStress' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the plots
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the Panoply graphical interface (menus, plot windows, scale adjustments) rather than using a Python script or CLI tool to generate the images.

integrity.description: The saved PNG files are genuine Panoply exports
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported images or plot windows match the visual style of Panoply plots (e.g., Panoply default map projections, color bars, and title fonts).

integrity.description: The agent manually created the thermal stress report
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) is seen open with the agent actively writing or saving the 'thermal_stress_report.txt' file, rather than just copying a pre-made file from elsewhere.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires analyzing the 'sst' variable from '/home/ga/PanoplyData/sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc' using Panoply. The monitoring month is August (time index 7). The bleaching threshold is 28.2°C. The expected outputs must be saved in '/home/ga/Documents/ReefStress/' and include 'reef_stress_global_aug.png', 'reef_stress_hotspot.png', and 'thermal_stress_report.txt'. The report should identify the Caribbean (max ~30.05°C) and IPWP (max ~30.04°C) as thermally stressed regions.
